title: Independent AI Consultant
description: You're an independent AI consultant working with multiple clients. You help businesses implement AI solutions — from strategy and architecture to hands-on model building and deployment. You set your own rates, choose your projects, and build a reputation as a specialist.

schedule.activity: Morning routine — review client Slack channels, check overnight model training results
Client A: Build a RAG pipeline for their internal knowledge base
Client B: Strategy call — assess whether they need custom fine-tuning or if prompt engineering is sufficient
Write a technical proposal for a new client — scope, architecture, timeline, pricing
Lunch and walk — protect your energy, you're the entire team
Client A: Deploy the RAG pipeline to production, set up monitoring
Professional development — take an online course module, read papers, experiment with new tools
Client C: Review and optimise their model inference costs — quantisation, batching, caching
Admin: send invoices, update project trackers, follow up on a late payment
Network: coffee with a former colleague who might refer a project
Wrap up: update client project boards, plan tomorrow

responsibilities: Assess client AI readiness and recommend solutions
Architect and build AI systems end-to-end
Write proposals, scope projects, negotiate contracts
Stay current with rapidly evolving AI tools and models
Handle your own business admin (invoicing, tax, insurance)
Build and maintain your professional network
Build your reputation — your own business still needs visibility and trust
challenges: Inconsistent income — feast or famine cycles
Staying current when the field moves this fast
Scope creep from clients who keep adding requirements
Being the sole decision-maker with no team to consult
Explaining complex AI concepts to non-technical clients
Managing client expectations around what AI can and can't do
rewards: Complete freedom over schedule, clients, and technology choices
Highest earning potential in AI — rates of $200-400/hour are common
Variety — every client is a different problem domain
Build deep expertise across multiple industries
No corporate politics or pointless meetings
careerPath: AI Engineer at a company (2-5 years to build skills and reputation)
Start consulting part-time alongside your job
Scale to a boutique AI consultancy with 2-4 engineers
Or: become a fractional CTO/Head of AI for multiple companies
